rulings.Case Title: THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES, IN THEIR CAPACITIES VS. A&R CASEWORK AND CONSTRUCTION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Petition To Confirm Arb Award; Index
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, Line 7: PETITIONER THE BOARD OF TRUSTEES, in their capacities as Trustees of the Carpenters Health and Welfare Fund for California's Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Off calendar. Petitioner did not timely file proof of service for the "Amended Petition to Confirm Arbitration Awards." (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(c).) On March 21, 2014, Petitioners filed an "Amended Petition to Confirm Arbitration Awards" to confirm two arbitration awards for: (1) Grievance 13-031 conducted on October 8, 2013 (Am. Petition, 8); and (2) Grievance 13-044 conducted on November 12, 2013 (Id. at 9). (Am. Petition, p. 4:10-1. See id. at Exhibits B, C.) Petitioner has not demonstrated that this Amended Petition was served on Respondent. On March 26, 2014, Petitioner filed proof of service, dated March 17, 2014, demonstrating substitute service on March 16, 2014 of filings related to the "Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award," but not the "Amended Petition to Confirm Arbitration Awards." No other proof of service has been filed demonstrating service of the "Amended Petition to Confirm Arbitration Awards." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BITDEFENDER SRL, A ROMANIAN LIMITED LIABILITY VS. 5381 PARTNERS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, Line 18: Counsel for DEFENDANT 5381 PARTNERS LLC'S, a Delaware limited liability company, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Hearing required. The proof of service filed April 7, 2014 does not indicate the name of the person served on behalf of 5381 Partners LLC. (See para. 5(a), requiring declarant to state the "name of person or persons" served; and the Declaration of Messenger on page 2 of the Proof of Service, which refers to delivery "to the persons at the addresses listed in item 5.") Attorney seeking to withdraw must bring further proof of service to the hearing to establish that 5381 Partners LLC has been served with this motion. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALMA ARCINIEGAS VS. CORE CARE CENTER LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, Line 12: DEFENDANT KANE IR (BUSINESS ASSOCIATION, FORM UNKNOWN), and ROBERT KANE, DC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike punitive damages claims (page 11, paragraph 55 and page 12, paragraph 3 of the prayer of the Complaint) is granted with ten days leave to amend to allege facts, if plaintiff can do so in good faith, sufficient to show (1) that the allegations against moving defendants do not fall within CCP section 425.13, and (2) an intent to cause harm as required by Civil Code § 3294(c). The conclusory allegations in the complaint are not sufficient. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT, A VS. AXIS INSURANCE COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 30, 2014, Line 13: DEFENDANT AXIS SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the first cause of action. Overruled as to the second cause of action. For the first cause of action for declaratory relief, Plaintiff concedes that it has not pled sufficient facts to allege a present duty to defend and requests leave to amend. (Opp., p. 2:6-17.) For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege equitable contribution. "Th[e] right of equitable contribution belongs to each insurer individually. It is not based on any right of subrogation to the rights of the insured, and is not equivalent to " 'standing in the shoes' " of the insured." (Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Maryland Cas. Co. (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 1279, 1294.) The second cause of action is not uncertain. (CCP § 430.10(f).) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
